description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched gable roof with multiple intersecting sections, covered in textured shingles. The front facade is defined by a deep, covered porch supported by sturdy columns, with a central stairway leading up to the entrance. The exterior walls are clad in a reddish-brown brick, and the windows are arranged in a balanced, symmetrical pattern on the front, with fewer and more irregularly placed windows on the sides. The structure sits on a rectangular plot with a driveway running along one side and a grassy yard in front. The roof overhangs provide shade and architectural interest, while the rear and side elevations are simpler, with minimal ornamentation. The overall design is solid and inviting, typical of early 20th-century American Craftsman bungalows.
description: A brick Craftsman-style bungalow with a wide gable roof, deep front porch, and symmetrical front windows, set on a rectangular lot with a driveway and grassy yard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the deep covered porch, central entrance, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the brick wall, minimal windows, and the side extension of the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simple brick facade, small central window, and no porch.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with minimal windows and a clear view of the driveway.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows, irregular side and rear windows
